reasoning: The Polymarket consensus probability was 54% on May 1, 2025. At that time, Bitcoin was trading around $96,000. Given 8 months remaining to reach the $120,000 target, a 54% "touch" probability implied by the market precisely corresponds to an annualized volatility of ~45% (using a simplified zero-drift one-touch barrier option model). Since May 1, Bitcoin has seen a robust rally, breaking out above $100k and trading at roughly $103,500 to $104,000 as of May 10-11, 2025. Re-evaluating the same 45% volatility model with the updated spot price of $104k and the slightly abridged timeline (~7.6 months remaining) yields a new touch probability of ~69%. Furthermore, earlier in 2025 (January), BTC had already set an ATH of $108,099, proving that it can sustain momentum well past the $100k psychological level line. The required appreciation from current levels is only ~15.3%, which is considered a routine swing in cryptocurrency markets over a multimonth period. Overall, the significant positive shift in spot price provides strong mathematical and momentum-based justification to update the 54% base probability to approximately 69%.
